etymology.description: semantic 齒(齒) (chǐ, "tooth") + phonetic 令(令) (lìng) -> meaning related to counting years
etymology.interpretation: The character 齡(齡) (líng, "age") is formed by combining 齒(齒) (chǐ, "tooth"), which represents 'age' through the symbolism of teeth as a marker of maturity, with the phonetic 令(令) (lìng), which contributes the sound. Teeth are often associated with stages of life, lending themselves naturally to the concept of age or duration.
etymology.simplification: Simplified to 龄 by changing 齒(齒) (chǐ, "tooth") to its simplified form 齿(齿).

examples.chinese: 她的年龄不大(她的年齡不大)。
他的实际年龄比看起来要大(他的實際年齡比看起來要大)。
examples.pinyin: Tā de shíjì niánlíng bǐ kàn qǐlái yào dà.
examples.english: His actual age is older than he looks.

examples.chinese: 这份工作的最低年龄要求是十八岁(這份工作的最低年齡要求是十八歲)。
examples.pinyin: Zhè fèn gōngzuò de zuìdī niánlíng yāoqiú shì shíbā suì.
examples.english: The minimum age requirement for this job is eighteen years old.
